prompt: repalce the button with the pot, that controls the number on 7segment display

trace.text: I'll replace the button with a potentiometer that controls the display value directly.
Now I'll replace the button with a potentiometer and update the sketch to read analog input:

toolResult.summary: Removed 1 component(s), Added 1 component(s), Created 4 wire(s), Updated sketch

trace.text:
Perfect! ✅ **Potentiometer control implemented.**

## Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Purpose |
|-------------|-----------|---------|
| A0 (D14) | Potentiometer | Analog input (signal) |
| D2 | 7-Segment | Segment A |
| D3 | 7-Segment | Segment B |
| D4 | 7-Segment | Segment C |
| D5 | 7-Segment | Segment D |
| D6 | 7-Segment | Segment E |
| D7 | 7-Segment | Segment F |
| D8 | 7-Segment | Segment G |
| 5V | Potentiometer | Power |
| GND | 7-Segment | Common cathode |
| GND | Potentiometer | Ground |

## How It Works

The potentiometer's middle pin sends an analog signal (0–1023) to A0. The sketch maps this range to 0–9 and displays the corresponding digit on the 7-segment display in real-time. Turn the pot knob to change the displayed number smoothly across all 10 digits.
